title: What is a prediction market?
hook: Betting on the future  - without a bookie

slides.headline: A prediction market lets you buy shares in outcomes
slides.body: Think: 'Will Trump win in 2028?' You buy YES at 40c. If he wins, you get $1. If he loses, you lose 40c. That's it.

slides.headline: The price IS the probability
slides.body: If YES shares trade at 73c, the market thinks there's a 73% chance it happens. Thousands of traders set this price  - not one bookie.

slides.headline: No overround. No bookie margin.
slides.body: Paddy Power takes 5-15% before you even start. Prediction markets? The odds add up to 100%. You get the fair price.

slides.headline: You can trade out any time
slides.body: Bought YES at 30c and it's now 70c? Sell your shares and pocket the profit. No need to wait for the event to finish.

caption_template:
Your bookie doesn't want you to know about this.

Prediction markets let you trade on real-world events  - elections, economics, sport  - at fair odds with zero margin.

Link in bio to start trading 👇

title: How Polymarket actually works

slides.headline: Step 1: Find a market
slides.body: Browse thousands of markets  - politics, crypto, sport, culture. Each one is a Yes/No question about a future event.

slides.headline: Step 2: Buy shares
slides.body: YES shares pay $1 if the event happens. NO shares pay $1 if it doesn't. The price you pay = the implied probability.

slides.headline: Step 3: Watch or trade
slides.body: Hold until the event resolves, or sell your shares early if the price moves in your favour. It's like a stock market for events.

slides.headline: Step 4: Get paid
slides.body: When the event resolves, winning shares pay out $1 each. Losers go to $0. Funds go straight to your wallet.

caption_template:
Polymarket in 60 seconds.

No bookie. No overround. Just you vs the market.

Link in bio to try it yourself 👇

title: Why prediction markets beat polls
hook: Polls ask opinions. Markets ask for money.

slides.headline: Polls are free to answer wrong
slides.body: When someone fills out a poll, they lose nothing by lying, guessing, or virtue signalling. There's no cost to being wrong.

slides.headline: Markets make you put skin in the game
slides.body: When you buy shares at 60c, you're risking real money on your belief. That changes how carefully you think.

slides.headline: The track record speaks for itself
slides.body: Prediction markets called Trump 2024 months before polls caught up. They spotted Brexit. They nailed COVID timelines.

slides.headline: The wisdom of crowds is real
slides.body: Thousands of traders with different information sources, all competing to be right. The result? The best forecast available anywhere.

caption_template:
Polls said Hillary would win. Markets said it was a coin flip.

Prediction markets have been embarrassing pollsters for years. Here's why.

Link in bio to see what the market thinks right now 👇

title: How to fund Polymarket from Ireland
hook: Yes, it works here. Here's how.

slides.headline: Step 1: Get a crypto wallet
slides.body: Download MetaMask or Coinbase Wallet. It takes 2 minutes. This is your gateway to Polymarket.

slides.headline: Step 2: Buy USDC
slides.body: Use Coinbase, Revolut, or Binance to buy USDC (a stablecoin pegged 1:1 to the US dollar). Irish accounts work fine.

slides.headline: Step 3: Deposit to Polymarket
slides.body: Connect your wallet to Polymarket and deposit USDC. You can also deposit directly with a card on Polymarket's site.

slides.headline: Step 4: Start trading
slides.body: You're in. Browse markets, buy shares, and start making money from your opinions. Withdrawals go back to your wallet.

caption_template:
Every second DM I get: 'does this work in Ireland?'

Yes. Here's how to get set up in 5 minutes.

title: The €1 rule — how prediction market payouts work

slides.headline: The only rule you need to know
slides.body: Every YES share pays $1 if the event happens. Every NO share pays $1 if it doesn't. That's the entire system.

slides.headline: The price = the probability
slides.body: If YES trades at 73c, the market says 73% chance. Buy at 73c, win $1 = 27c profit. Or buy NO at 27c and win $1 if it doesn't happen.

slides.headline: YES + NO always = $1
slides.body: That means 0% overround. No bookie margin. The full dollar goes to whoever is right.

slides.headline: You can sell early
slides.body: Bought YES at 30c and it's now 70c? Sell and pocket 40c profit without waiting for the event. It's a market, not a bet slip.

caption_template:
The maths behind Polymarket is embarrassingly simple.

Buy at 30c. Win = $1. Lose = $0. No bookie cut.

Link in bio to try it 👇

title: Why the crowd is smarter than your bookie

slides.headline: Your bookie employs one team to set odds
slides.body: A small group of traders and algorithms decide what price you get. Their job is to make the bookie money, not to be accurate.

slides.headline: Polymarket has thousands of independent traders
slides.body: Each one brings different information  - insiders, analysts, data nerds, domain experts. The price reflects ALL of their knowledge.

slides.headline: Bookies add margin. Markets don't.
slides.body: Paddy Power's overround means their odds are deliberately wrong. They shade prices to guarantee profit regardless of the outcome.

slides.headline: The result? Markets are more accurate.
slides.body: When prediction markets say 70%, it happens ~70% of the time. Bookies can't say the same  - because accuracy was never their goal.

caption_template:
Paddy Power employs 20 people to set your odds.

Polymarket has 10,000+ traders competing to be right.

Who do you trust?

Link in bio 👇

title: Polymarket vs Paddy Power — full breakdown

slides.headline: Fees: Paddy Power takes 5-15%
slides.body: The overround is built into every market. You're paying a hidden tax on every single bet. Polymarket? 0% overround.

slides.headline: Cashout: Paddy Power decides your price
slides.body: Their 'cash out' feature gives you whatever they feel like. On Polymarket, you sell on the open market at the real price.

slides.headline: Markets: Paddy Power picks what you can bet on
slides.body: Polymarket has thousands of markets  - elections, AI, economics, crypto, geopolitics. If people care about it, there's probably a market.

slides.headline: Transparency: Paddy Power is a black box
slides.body: Polymarket runs on blockchain. Every trade is public. Every resolution is verifiable. Try getting that from a bookie.

caption_template:
Paddy Power vs Polymarket.

One takes 10% and bans you for winning. The other charges nothing and lets anyone trade.
